questions.question: Calculate the perimeter of a rectangle with a length of 12 cm and a breadth of 8 cm.
questions.solution: Perimeter = $2 \times (\text{length} + \text{breadth}) = 2 \times (12 + 8) = 2 \times 20 = 40$ cm.

questions.question: What is the perimeter of a square whose side measures 2.5 m?
questions.solution: Perimeter of square = $4 \times \text{side} = 4 \times 2.5 = 10$ m.

questions.question: A triangular playground has sides of lengths 15 m, 20 m, and 25 m. Find its perimeter.
questions.solution: Perimeter = $15 + 20 + 25 = 60$ m.

questions.question: What is the cost of fencing a rectangular plot of length 200 m and width 150 m at the rate of Rs. 30 per metre?
questions.solution: Perimeter = $2 \times (200 + 150) = 2 \times 350 = 700$ m. Total cost = $700 \times 30 = 21,000$.

questions.question: A piece of wire 48 cm long is bent to form an equilateral triangle. What will be the length of each side?
questions.solution: An equilateral triangle has 3 equal sides. Side length = $48 \div 3 = 16$ cm.

questions.question: If a rectangle has an area of 300 sq m and its length is 20 m, find its width.
questions.solution: Area = $\text{length} \times \text{width}$. So, $300 = 20 \times \text{width}$. Width = $300 \div 20 = 15$ m.

questions.question: A square hall has an area of 144 sq m. What is the length of one side of the hall?
questions.solution: Area of square = $\text{side}^2$. Since $12 \times 12 = 144$, the side length is 12 m.

questions.question: Find the area of a square whose perimeter is 32 cm.
questions.solution: 1. Side length = Perimeter $\div 4 = 32 \div 4 = 8$ cm. 2. Area = $\text{side}^2 = 8 \times 8 = 64$ sq cm.

questions.question: A room's floor is 6 m long and 4 m wide. A square carpet of side 3 m is laid in the center. Find the area of the floor uncovered by the carpet.
questions.solution: 1. Area of floor = $6 \times 4 = 24$ sq m. 2. Area of carpet = $3 \times 3 = 9$ sq m. 3. Uncovered area = $24 - 9 = 15$ sq m.

questions.question: A rectangular garden is 25 m long and 18 m wide. Four square flower beds, each of side 2 m, are dug at the four corners. Find the area of the remaining part of the garden.
questions.solution: 1. Total area = $25 \times 18 = 450$ sq m. 2. Area of one flower bed = $2 \times 2 = 4$ sq m. 3. Total area of 4 beds = $4 \times 4 = 16$ sq m. 4. Remaining area = $450 - 16 = 434$ sq m.

questions.question: A wire is in the shape of a rectangle with length 10 cm and breadth 6 cm. If the wire is straightened and then bent into a square, what will be the side of the square?
questions.solution: 1. Length of wire (Perimeter of rectangle) = $2 \times (10 + 6) = 32$ cm. 2. Side of square = $32 \div 4 = 8$ cm.

questions.question: What happens to the area of a rectangle if its length is doubled while its width remains the same?
questions.solution: Area = $l \times w$. If length becomes $2l$, the new area is $2l \times w = 2 \times (l \times w)$.

questions.question: A rectangle ABCD has an area of 60 sq cm. If a diagonal AC divides it into two triangles, what is the area of triangle ABC?
questions.solution: The diagonal of a rectangle divides it into two triangles of equal area. Area of triangle = $\text{Area of rectangle} \div 2 = 60 \div 2 = 30$ sq cm.

questions.question: Using 16 unit squares, what is the smallest possible perimeter of a figure formed by connecting them along full sides?
questions.solution: The smallest perimeter for a fixed area is achieved by a square. For 16 sq units, a $4 \times 4$ square has a perimeter of $4 \times 4 = 16$ units.

questions.question: Using 16 unit squares, what is the largest possible perimeter of a single connected figure?
questions.solution: The largest perimeter is achieved by a long $16 \times 1$ rectangle. Perimeter = $2 \times (16 + 1) = 34$ units.

questions.question: Tangrams: If Shape C and Shape E are identical and together cover Shape D exactly, what is the relationship between their areas?
questions.answer: Area of D is twice the area of C.
questions.solution: Since Shape D = Shape C + Shape E, and C = E, then Area of D = $2 \times$ Area of C.

questions.question: A square sheet of side 20 cm is folded into two equal rectangles. What is the perimeter of each resulting rectangle?
questions.solution: Folding side 20 cm in half gives a rectangle with length 20 cm and breadth 10 cm. Perimeter = $2 \times (20 + 10) = 60$ cm.

questions.question: What is the cost of tiling a room of $5 \text{ m} \times 4 \text{ m}$ at the rate of Rs. 10 per square metre?
questions.solution: Area = $5 \times 4 = 20$ sq m. Cost = $20 \times 10 = 200$.

questions.question: A rectangular park of $100 \text{ m} \times 80 \text{ m}$ needs to be fenced with 3 rounds of wire. What is the total length of wire required?
questions.solution: 1. Perimeter = $2 \times (100 + 80) = 360$ m. 2. Total wire for 3 rounds = $3 \times 360 = 1080$ m.

questions.question: An irregular shape on a grid covers 4 full squares, 2 squares that are more than half-covered, and 3 squares that are less than half-covered. Estimate its area.
questions.solution: Count full squares (4) + squares more than half (2) + ignore those less than half (0). Area = $4 + 2 = 6$ square units.
